summary: The explicit solvent validation plan has evolved from REVISE (Cycle 1) → ACCEPT_WITH_CONDITIONS (Cycle 2) → APPROVE (Cycle 3). All critical concerns from Cycle 1 are resolved: (1) P1a is now explicit with Option A verified (1UAO.pdb exists; test file present with @pytest.mark.integration), (2) SETTLE scalar path confirmed complete (commit 6a47281, settle_langevin function exists), (3) P2a metrics are detailed and realistic with distribution-level approach. All four Cycle 2 conditions have been incorporated into the plan: pre-execution checklist is concrete (§6b), P3/P4a decoupling is explicit in dependency graph, P2a CPU/GPU scope is bounded with <2% variance acceptance, and P5a timing granularity is documented. The revised comprehensive plan is execution-ready. Timeline is credible (5–6 weeks, 3–4 week critical path) with grounded effort estimates and clear fallback paths. No new gaps introduced by revisions. Plan maintains full alignment with Phase 3/4/6/8/9 and SETTLE integration plans. Pre-execution checklist is sufficient de-risking (4 items, all checkboxes provided).

cycle_1_findings: P1a solvated protein test vague — existing test unclear; reuse vs new creation undefined
SETTLE scalar path status ambiguous — effort estimate hides scope
P2a metrics incomplete — tolerance policy not specified; RNG/platform handling unclear
cycle_1_resolution: ✓ P1a clarified to Option A (existing 1UAO test, 1 week) vs Option B (new minimal test, +1 week)
✓ SETTLE status confirmed complete (commit 6a47281, settle_langevin); P1b is documentation-only
✓ P2a metrics table added (mean T 295–305K, 10 ps duration, 5 ps window, float64, distribution-level)
cycle_2_conditions: C1: P1a CI Integration Path — Verify 1UAO test runs locally before execution
C2: P3 Profiling vs P4a Start — Decouple profiling gate from benchmarking start
C3: P2a CPU/GPU Scope — Bound acceptable platform variance (<2% float64)
C4: P5a Start Granularity — Clarify runbook can draft once P1a direction chosen
cycle_2_incorporation_status: ✓ C1 incorporated: §6b Pre-Execution Checklist added; 1UAO.pdb verified (found at data/pdb/1UAO.pdb)
✓ C2 incorporated: Dependency graph and timeline section rewrote P3 as independent (not blocking P4a)
✓ C3 incorporated: P2a metrics table now specifies GPU required for CI, CPU optional; <2% variance bound documented
✓ C4 incorporated: §3 Dependencies section clarifies P5a can draft Week 2 once P1a choice made

codebase_fact_checks.evidence: ls -la data/pdb/1UAO*.pdb returns -rw-r--r-- 1 marielle marielle 217080 Feb 26 07:23 data/pdb/1UAO.pdb
File found at tests/physics/test_explicit_solvation_parity.py; test class TestOpenMMSolvationParity marked with @pytest.mark.integration
git log shows commit 6a47281 'feat: Integrate SETTLE for rigid water and optimize GB NL mask gathering'; settle_langevin function exists in src/prolix/physics/settle.py:410
tests/physics/conftest.py lines 140–152 define REGRESSION_EXPLICIT_PME dict with pme_alpha_per_angstrom=0.34, pme_grid_points=32, cutoff_angstrom=9.0

codebase_fact_checks.fact: P1b already has extractable regression config
codebase_fact_checks.evidence: conftest.py REGRESSION_EXPLICIT_PME is centralized and reusable; P1b work is creating YAML wrapper and CI gate (2–3 days realistic)

items.title: P1a CI Integration Path
criteria: Go decision: If test passes → P1a Option A ready (1 week); if fails → debug or fallback to Option B
items.assessment: Concrete. Developer can execute immediately. 1UAO.pdb is verified; test file exists. OpenMM optional dep installation is the only variable (should be straightforward on target platform).

items.title: Decouple P3 & P4a
criteria: Updated plan reflects decoupling (yes, done in §3 Dependencies & Sequencing)
Team agrees: Benchmarking is independent of optimization gate
P3 outcome may trigger future optimization work (Phase 6+); does not block P4a
items.assessment: Concrete. P3/P4a independence is explicitly shown in mermaid graph (Section 3). Timeline updated to show P4a starting Week 3 regardless of P3 outcome. No ambiguity.

items.title: P2a CPU/GPU Scope
criteria: Decision: GPU required for CI; CPU optional for local dev
Variance bound: Accept <2% float64 platform differences
Document in test docstring if GPU vs. CPU shows variance
items.assessment: Concrete. Metrics table (§2 Phase P2, P2a subsection) specifies GPU required for CI, CPU optional. <2% tolerance is documented. Clear acceptance criteria.

items.title: P5a Start Condition
criteria: Updated plan shows P5a can draft by Week 2 (parallel with P1a execution)
Runbook will reference 1UAO or Ala10 example based on P1a outcome
items.assessment: Concrete. §3 Dependencies section clarifies P5a drafting timeline. Runbook example choice is deferred to P1a outcome (Option A → 1UAO; Option B → Ala10). No ambiguity.

pre_execution_checklist.de_risking_sufficiency: HIGH. Checklist covers all four Cycle 2 conditional items. Each item has concrete acceptance criteria. Developer can pick up checklist and begin execution. 1UAO availability is verified; test infrastructure is in place. OpenMM CI integration is the only execution risk (unknown effort), but plan accommodates fallback (Option B) if OpenMM setup is slower than expected.

timeline_credibility.critical_path: P1a (1 wk) → P1b (3 days overlap) → P5a (1 wk) → P5b (1 wk) = 3.4 weeks elapsed + buffer = 4–5 weeks realistic
timeline_credibility.total_project_time: 5–6 weeks (P2a 2–3 weeks, P3 1 week run in parallel; non-blocking)
timeline_credibility.fallback_scenario: If P1a CI setup + 1UAO test execution requires debugging (Option B pathway): +1 week → 6–7 weeks total. Plan accommodates this; not a surprise.

effort_estimates_assessment.assessment: GROUNDED. Test file and PDB exist. Work is verification + documentation + CI gating. Fallback (Ala10 + 500 waters minimal test) is straightforward (scaffold + fixture). Both 1-week estimates are credible.

REALISTIC. Regression config already exists (REGRESSION_EXPLICIT_PME in conftest.py). Work is: (1) create regression_pme_config.yaml wrapper, (2) update tests to load it, (3) add CI gate. Low implementation risk.

CREDIBLE. Metrics table is specific and realistic (10 ps duration, 5 ps sampling window, mean T tolerance ±1.67%). Test infrastructure exists (NVE/NVT tests already in repo). New work is integrating Langevin integrator, logging, and statistical comparison. Distribution-level approach avoids RNG matching complexity. 2–3 weeks is appropriate; could slip 1 week if platform testing diverges.

GROUNDED. Profiling gate doc already exists (spatial_sorting_profile_gate.md). Work is executing profiling artifact (1–2 days), generating JSON report (1 day), executing decision tree (2–3 days). No code implementation required. 1 week is realistic.

CREDIBLE. SLURM templates exist (scripts/slurm/bench_*.slurm). Work is parameterization (1 day), CI integration (2–3 days), documentation (2–3 days). Templates are straightforward; not a new implementation effort.

REALISTIC. Runbook is text document. Work is drafting happy path (2–3 days), adding code examples (2 days), local validation (2 days). Examples can reuse existing test fixtures (low implementation risk).

timeline_credibility.parallelization_realism: CORRECT. P1/P2/P3 can overlap (P1a 1–2 weeks, P2a 2–3 weeks run in parallel). P4a independent (no P3 outcome dependency). P5a sequential after P1 (correct; must know which test was used for example). Dependency graph is accurate.

concern_p1a_solvated_protein_clarity.cycle_1_issue: Plan mentioned creating new solvated protein test but was unclear whether to reuse existing test or write new artifact.
concern_p1a_solvated_protein_clarity.cycle_2_clarification: Distinguished Option A (existing 1UAO test, 1 week) vs Option B (new minimal test, +1 week). Pre-execution validation checklist added to verify 1UAO.pdb availability.
concern_p1a_solvated_protein_clarity.cycle_3_verification: 1UAO.pdb confirmed in repo (data/pdb/1UAO.pdb, 217KB, committed Feb 26). Test file exists (test_explicit_solvation_parity.py, 1UAO + 5000 waters). @pytest.mark.integration present. Plan now EXPLICIT: pursue Option A (1 week) as primary path; fallback to Option B if 1UAO test fails locally.

concern_settle_scalar_path_status.cycle_1_issue: Unclear whether settle_rattle_langevin is merged; P1b effort estimate (2–3 days) may be hiding broader scope.
concern_settle_scalar_path_status.cycle_2_clarification: Verified: SETTLE scalar path is complete (commit 6a47281, 'feat: Integrate SETTLE for rigid water and optimize GB NL mask gathering'). Function is settle_langevin (not settle_rattle_langevin). P1b is purely documentation (extract PME config, create CI gate).
concern_settle_scalar_path_status.cycle_3_verification: settle_langevin function confirmed in src/prolix/physics/settle.py:410. REGRESSION_EXPLICIT_PME fixture confirmed in tests/physics/conftest.py. P1b work scope is: (1) create regression_pme_config.yaml, (2) update tests to load it, (3) add CI gate. 2–3 days estimate is REALISTIC. No implementation blocker; pure documentation/tooling work.

concern_p2a_metric_incompleteness.cycle_1_issue: Plan says 'mean T within 5%' without specifying: system size, duration, precision, sampling window, platform coverage, RNG strategy.
concern_p2a_metric_incompleteness.cycle_2_clarification: Detailed metrics table added (§2 Phase P2, P2a subsection): 4-charge PME solvated box (~100 atoms), 10 ps total, 5 ps sampling window (post-equilibration), temperature tolerance 295–305K (±1.67% at 300K), float64 precision, GPU required for CI (CPU optional), distribution-level comparison (mean T, variance) NOT trajectory matching.
concern_p2a_metric_incompleteness.cycle_3_verification: Metrics table is comprehensive and realistic. System size (~100 atoms) is representative but CI-friendly. Duration (10 ps = 500 steps × 20 fs) is reasonable for CI runtime (~2–3 min per run). Sampling window (5 ps) is standard for thermostat equilibration. Temperature tolerance (±1.67%) is well-calibrated for Langevin integrators. Distribution-level approach avoids RNG matching complexity while ensuring integrator correctness. CPU/GPU scope is bounded (GPU required for CI, CPU optional for dev; <2% variance acceptable).

c1_p1a_ci_integration_path.cycle_2_issue: Plan assumes 'add test to CI gate: weekly run with OpenMM dep' but does not specify CI workflow type, OpenMM installation, or 1UAO.pdb commit strategy.
c1_p1a_ci_integration_path.cycle_2_recommendation: Create CI integration checklist before execution.
c1_p1a_ci_integration_path.evidence: §6b Pre-Execution Validation Checklist, item C1, specifies: (1) Verify 1UAO.pdb exists (VERIFIED: found), (2) Run test locally with pytest -m openmm, (3) Confirm markers (@pytest.mark.integration @pytest.mark.openmm), (4) Go/No-Go decision. If test passes locally → Option A ready (1 week); if fails → debug or fallback to Option B.
c1_p1a_ci_integration_path.residual_risk: LOW. Checklist is concrete. 1UAO.pdb is verified. OpenMM optional dep installation is the only variable; straightforward on standard environments. If OpenMM setup is complex on target platform, fallback (Option B) adds 1 week, which plan accommodates.

c2_p3_profiling_incorrectly_gated.cycle_2_issue: Timeline shows 'P4a (if P3 gates optimization)' implying P4a waits for P3, but profiling is a *measurement tool* for optimization decisions, not a *prerequisite for benchmarking*. Artificial sequencing delays P4a.
c2_p3_profiling_incorrectly_gated.cycle_2_recommendation: Decouple P3 and P4a. P3 runs independently (weeks 2–3), produces recommendation. P4a starts week 3 *regardless* of P3 outcome.
c2_p3_profiling_incorrectly_gated.evidence: §3 Dependencies & Sequencing (mermaid graph and text) now clearly shows: P3 outputs a decision ('Morton needed? Yes/No'); P4a (SLURM wiring, smoke test, schema) is independent and starts Week 3 regardless. Timeline Section 6 (Table and Critical Path Analysis) explicitly states: 'P3 (profiling gate; decision only) informs whether Morton is needed, NOT whether benchmarking is needed. P4a should proceed regardless.'
c2_p3_profiling_incorrectly_gated.residual_risk: NONE. Decoupling is explicit in updated plan. No ambiguity.

c3_p2a_cpu_gpu_scope_vague.cycle_2_issue: Plan says 'CPU + GPU (validate on both if feasible)' without specifying: what 'feasible' means, expected variance, acceptance criteria if CPU/GPU diverge.
c3_p2a_cpu_gpu_scope_vague.cycle_2_recommendation: Add clarification: GPU required for CI; CPU optional for dev. Bound variance <2% in float64. If diverge >5%, investigate and document.
c3_p2a_cpu_gpu_scope_vague.evidence: P2a metrics table (§2 Phase P2, P2a subsection) now specifies: 'GPU (NVIDIA) required for CI; CPU testing optional (local dev sandbox). Expect <2% platform variance in float64. If diverge >5%, investigate precision/scheduler differences; document as known variance in protocol.' Clear, bounded, executable criteria.
c3_p2a_cpu_gpu_scope_vague.residual_risk: NONE. Scope is explicit. Acceptable variance is bounded. Decision criteria for failure cases are documented.

c4_p5a_start_granularity.cycle_2_issue: Plan says P5a requires 'P1a + P1b complete' but P5a could start once P1a *direction* is chosen (Option A/B), even if test still executing.
c4_p5a_start_granularity.cycle_2_recommendation: Optional refinement: Document P5a can begin once P1a direction chosen, with final sign-off after P1a test passing + P1b complete.
c4_p5a_start_granularity.evidence: §3 Dependencies & Sequencing now states: 'P5a (runbook update): Start Condition — Can begin once P1a choice is made (not blocked by P1a completion). Drafting can start Week 2. Final P5a completion requires P1a test passing + P1b config file.' This is a minor optimization but correctly documented.
c4_p5a_start_granularity.residual_risk: NONE. Timing is now explicit. No impact on critical path (overlap is <1 week).

vs_phase_3_4_6_8_9_approved_plan.notes: Plan correctly references prior Phase 3/4/6/8/9 plan as superseded by this comprehensive plan. Phase 6 profiling gate is integrated into P3 (with decision tree inlined). Phase 8 benchmarking is reflected in P4a/P4b. Phase 9 runbook updates are P5a. No new constraints introduced; inheritance is clean.

vs_settle_integration_plan.notes: SETTLE scalar path (commit 6a47281) is correctly marked as complete. Plan does not propose reimplementing SETTLE. P1b is correctly identified as documentation-only (extract PME config, CI gate). No scope creep.

vs_current_implementation_snapshot.notes: Plan correctly reflects current state: PME + NL + SETTLE + RATTLE integrated; RF/DSF opt-in; cell-list not default; no Morton ordering yet. All claims match codebase facts verified in Cycle 3.

risks_closed_by_cycle_revisions: P1a clarity — Option A/B fork is now explicit with decision point (verify 1UAO.pdb)
SETTLE implementation scope — Verified complete; P1b is documentation-only (no hidden work)
P2a metric underspecification — Detailed tolerance table + distribution-level approach eliminates RNG matching complexity
P3/P4a artificial coupling — Decoupled in timeline; P4a independent (profiling informs optimization, not benchmarking)
P2a CPU/GPU ambiguity — Bounded platform variance <2% float64; GPU required for CI, CPU optional for dev

remaining_execution_risks.risk: OpenMM optional dep installation in CI (platform-specific builds)
remaining_execution_risks.mitigation: Pre-execution checklist includes local test run (pytest -m openmm) to verify OpenMM is functional. If OpenMM setup is complex, fallback to Option B (new minimal test without OpenMM dependency) adds 1 week.
remaining_execution_risks.likelihood: MEDIUM (OpenMM is well-tested library, but build can be finicky on some CI runners)
remaining_execution_risks.impact_if_occurs: Delays P1a by 1–2 days for debugging; option B reduces scope to self-contained test.

remaining_execution_risks.risk: Langevin parity test platform variance >5% on CPU vs GPU
remaining_execution_risks.mitigation: Plan explicitly documents acceptable variance <2%; if diverge >5%, investigate precision/scheduler differences and document as known variance. Does not block release (variance is acceptable for float64 systems).
remaining_execution_risks.likelihood: LOW (both use float64 precision; scheduler differences are typically <2%)
remaining_execution_risks.impact_if_occurs: No blocking impact; documented as platform note in release.

remaining_execution_risks.risk: SLURM cluster site-specific configuration delays P4a
remaining_execution_risks.mitigation: Plan explicitly states cluster setup is 'nice-to-have for scale'. Smoke tests run locally and suffice for CI gate. Can deploy without full cluster results.
remaining_execution_risks.likelihood: MEDIUM (SLURM setup is site-dependent; Engaging cluster may require user configuration)
remaining_execution_risks.impact_if_occurs: P4a automation delayed; smoke tests still provide release-gate quality.

contingency_paths: If P1a Option A (1UAO test) fails locally → fallback to Option B (minimal test, +1 week)
If P2a platform variance >5% → document as known variance, proceed with release
If SLURM cluster setup stalls → use smoke tests for CI gate; defer full cluster matrix to Phase 6+
If P3 profiling reveals scatter bottleneck (>15% time) → estimate Morton implementation (2–4 weeks) as separate backlog; does not block P4a

final_assessment.plan_quality: HIGH. Comprehensive, well-structured, detailed metrics, explicit dependencies, realistic effort estimates, clear fallback paths.
final_assessment.execution_readiness: EXECUTION-READY. All Cycle 1 critical concerns resolved. All Cycle 2 conditions incorporated. Pre-execution checklist is concrete and checkable. Developer can pick up plan and begin execution.
final_assessment.pre_execution_checklist_sufficiency: HIGH. §6b checklist covers all de-risking items: (1) 1UAO.pdb verification (✅ done), (2) local test run (executable), (3) P3/P4a decoupling agreement (done in plan), (4) P2a GPU/CPU scope (documented), (5) P5a timing granularity (documented). Checklist is not aspirational; it is a concrete set of executable items that de-risk the plan.
final_assessment.timeline_realism: CREDIBLE. 5–6 week total (3–4 week critical path) is achievable if: (1) 1UAO.pdb CI integration is straightforward (verified; OpenMM optional dep is the variable), (2) test execution + CI gating complete quickly (low risk; test infrastructure exists), (3) P2a distribution-level approach avoids RNG complexity (plan specifies this). Fallback +1 week for Option B is reasonable. Contingencies are identified; no surprises.
final_assessment.release_blocker_status: CLEAR. P1a (solvated protein parity) + P1b (PME regression config) + P5a (runbook) + P5b (release notes) are blocking. All have clear acceptance criteria. P2a/P3/P4 are high-quality assurance but not blocking release. This is correct prioritization.
final_assessment.hidden_dependencies: NONE IDENTIFIED. P1a and P1b are independent (both start Week 1). P2a/P3/P4 are parallel to critical path. P5a depends on P1 (correct; needs to know test/example). Mermaid graph is accurate.
final_assessment.recommendation: ✅ APPROVE. Plan is sound, execution-ready, and de-risked. Proceed with execution. Notify team: (1) start pre-execution checklist immediately (C1–C4 items), (2) verify 1UAO test runs locally before committing to 1-week estimate for P1a, (3) decouple P3/P4a understanding (profiling informs optimization, not benchmarking), (4) parallel P1/P2/P3 starting Week 1.

post_approval_follow_up: Execute pre-execution checklist (§6b, items C1–C4) before Week 1 starts. Gate on C1 local test run: if test passes → P1a Option A ready; if fails → investigate OpenMM setup or fallback to Option B.

Notify team: P3 profiling gate (decision tree output) does NOT block P4a start. P4a should begin Week 3 regardless of profiling outcome. Profiling may inform *future* optimization work; it does not gate benchmarking infrastructure.
Once P1a direction is chosen (Option A or B), P5a runbook drafting can begin (Week 2 parallel with P1a execution). Final sign-off of P5a requires P1a test passing + P1b complete.
Monitor P2a RNG/platform testing: if CPU vs GPU diverge >5%, document as known variance and proceed (variance is acceptable for float64 systems). Do not over-optimize for platform convergence.

If P3 profiling reveals scatter bottleneck (scatter_time_ms / total > 15% for N ≥ 5000): estimate Morton implementation cost (2–4 weeks) and add as separate Phase 6+ backlog item. Does not block release or P4a automation.
approval_gates_satisfied: ✅ Cycle 1 concerns resolved: P1a explicit, SETTLE complete, P2a metrics specified
✅ Cycle 2 conditions incorporated: C1–C4 all addressed in plan
✅ Pre-execution checklist is concrete and executable
✅ Timeline is credible (5–6 weeks) with grounded effort estimates
✅ No new gaps introduced by revisions
✅ Plan maintains alignment with Phase 3/4/6/8/9 and SETTLE integration plans
✅ Fallback paths are explicit (P1a Option B, P3 closure, P4a independence)
✅ Critical path is clear and unambiguous (P1a → P1b → P5a → P5b)
